title: Data Masking — Sampled PII Pattern Hits in Non-Production Test Indexes

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=pii_validation sourcetype=* earliest=-24h
| head 50000
| regex _raw="(?i)\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b"
| stats count as ssn_like_hits by sourcetype, host
| where ssn_like_hits>0
description: Scans a controlled non-production sample for raw SSN-like patterns to validate masking and access restrictions before production promotion.
value: Scans a controlled non-production sample for raw SSN-like patterns to validate masking and access restrictions before production promotion.
implementation: (1) Use synthetic data only in lab; (2) expand regex to local PII taxonomy; (3) document masking standard.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise search.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=pii_validation`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Use synthetic data only in lab; (2) expand regex to local PII taxonomy; (3) document masking standard.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=pii_validation sourcetype=* earliest=-24h
| head 50000
| regex _raw="(?i)\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b"
| stats count as ssn_like_hits by sourcetype, host
| where ssn_like_hits>0
```

Understanding this SPL

**Data Masking — Sampled PII Pattern Hits in Non-Production Test Indexes (A.8.11)** — Scans a controlled non-production sample for raw SSN-like patterns to validate masking and access restrictions before production promotion.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=pii_validation`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise search. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: pii_validation; **sourcetype**: *. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=pii_validation, sourcetype=*, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Limits the number of rows with `head`.
• Filters rows matching a pattern with `regex`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by sourcetype, host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where ssn_like_hits>0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Single value
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
